UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile, including personal preferences, family details, and settings for the PantryPal AI app. This entity's ID links to an external authentication system's user ID, as no authentication data is stored internally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This ID links to the external authentication system's user ID.

email.description: User's email address, used for communication and notifications.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for app interactions and AI audio responses (e.g., 'es-LA' for Latin American Spanish, 'en-US' for US English).

preferredVoiceType.description: The user's selected voice type for AI audio responses, allowing customization of accent and gender (e.g., 'Spanish_Latin_Female', 'English_US_Male').

familyMembers.description: A list of family members, detailing their age and relationship to the primary user, collected via AI voice interface.

cookingTimePreference.description: User's preference for the maximum time spent cooking (e.g., 'short' for under 30min, 'medium' for 30-60min, 'long' for over 60min).

recipeComplexityPreference.description: User's preference for the complexity level of recipes (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'advanced'), prioritizing practicality.

applianceIds.description: References to kitchen appliances the user has available. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Appliance)

dietaryRestrictionIds.description: References to dietary restrictions the user or family members follow (e.g., vegetarian, vegan). (Relationship: UserProfile N:N DietaryRestriction)

allergyIds.description: References to known food allergies for the user or family members (e.g., peanuts, lactose). (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Allergy)

tastePreferenceIds.description: References to general taste preferences of the user or family members (e.g., spicy, sweet, savory). (Relationship: UserProfile N:N TastePreference)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

Ingredient.title: Ingredient
Ingredient.description: Represents a canonical definition of a food ingredient, independent of specific pantry stock. This serves as a master list for all possible food items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ingredient entity.

name.description: The common name of the ingredient (e.g., 'Milk', 'Chicken Breast', 'Tomato Sauce').

description.description: A brief, descriptive summary of the ingredient.

category.description: The food category the ingredient belongs to (e.g., 'Dairy', 'Vegetable', 'Meat', 'Grain', 'Spice', 'Fruit').

commonUnits.description: A list of common units this ingredient is typically measured in (e.g., ['ml', 'liter', 'gram', 'kg', 'unit', 'cup']).

imageUrls.description: URLs to images representing this ingredient, potentially used for AI model training or user interface display.

PantryItem.title: PantryItem
PantryItem.description: Represents a specific instance of an ingredient currently available in a user's pantry or fridge, including its identified state and quantity. This is the dynamic inventory.

id.description: Unique identifier for this specific PantryItem instance.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this pantry item. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PantryItem)

ingredientId.description: Reference to the canonical Ingredient definition for this item. (Relationship: Ingredient 1:N PantryItem)

quantityDescription.description: A descriptive string representing the quantity, as identified by AI or provided by user voice input (e.g., 'a la mitad', '2 unidades', 'casi lleno').

estimatedQuantityValue.description: The estimated numerical quantity of the item, useful for recipe matching (e.g., 0.5 for 'half', 2 for '2 units', 200 for '200 grams').

estimatedQuantityUnit.description: The estimated unit for the numerical quantity (e.g., 'units', 'grams', 'ml', 'ounces', 'liters').

identifiedFrom.description: The source from which this item was identified (e.g., 'initialScan' from video/photo, 'receiptScan' from grocery ticket, 'voiceInput', 'manualAdd').

expirationDate.description: The estimated or actual expiration date of the item, if available.

lastScanOrVoiceUpdate.description: Timestamp of the last time this item's status or quantity was updated by an AI scan or voice command.

addedDate.description: Timestamp when this item was first added to the pantry.

status.description: Current status of the pantry item (e.g., 'available', 'consumed', 'low_quantity', 'expired').

Recipe.title: Recipe
Recipe.description: Defines a specific cooking recipe, including its ingredients, instructions, and metadata, generated or curated by the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Recipe entity.

description.description: A short, engaging description of the recipe.

instructions.description: An ordered list of steps to prepare the recipe.

prepTimeMinutes.description: The estimated preparation time for the recipe, in minutes.

cookTimeMinutes.description: The estimated cooking time for the recipe, in minutes.

servings.description: The number of servings the recipe yields.

complexityLevel.description: The complexity level of the recipe (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'advanced'), aligned with user preferences.

cuisineType.description: The culinary origin or style of the recipe (e.g., 'Italian', 'Mexican', 'Asian', 'Mediterranean').

imageUrls.description: URLs to images of the prepared recipe.

requiredIngredients.description: A list of ingredients required for the recipe, including their quantities. (Relationship: Recipe N:N Ingredient)

dietaryRestrictionIds.description: References to dietary restrictions this recipe adheres to or is suitable for. (Relationship: Recipe N:N DietaryRestriction)

applianceRequirements.description: References to kitchen appliances commonly required to prepare this recipe. (Relationship: Recipe N:N Appliance)

Appliance.title: Appliance
Appliance.description: Represents a common kitchen appliance that users might have and which could be relevant for recipe suggestions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appliance entity.

name.description: The name of the appliance (e.g., 'Oven', 'Microwave', 'Blender', 'Air Fryer', 'Stovetop').

description.description: A brief description of the appliance and its primary uses.

DietaryRestriction.title: DietaryRestriction
DietaryRestriction.description: Represents a specific dietary restriction or preference that users or family members may have (e.g., 'Vegetarian', 'Gluten-Free').

id.description: Unique identifier for the DietaryRestriction entity.

name.description: The name of the dietary restriction (e.g., 'Vegetarian', 'Vegan', 'Keto', 'Halal', 'Pescatarian').

description.description: A brief description of what this dietary restriction entails.

Allergy.title: Allergy
Allergy.description: Represents a specific food allergy that users or family members may have (e.g., 'Peanuts', 'Lactose').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Allergy entity.

description.description: A brief description of the allergy and its implications.

TastePreference.title: TastePreference
TastePreference.description: Represents a general taste preference or flavor profile that users or family members enjoy (e.g., 'Spicy', 'Sweet').

id.description: Unique identifier for the TastePreference entity.

name.description: The name of the taste preference (e.g., 'Spicy', 'Sweet', 'Savory', 'Umami', 'Mediterranean Flavors').

description.description: A brief description of the taste preference.

UserRecipeInteraction.title: UserRecipeInteraction
UserRecipeInteraction.description: Logs user interactions with suggested recipes (e.g., acceptance, rejection, cooking), providing valuable data for refining AI suggestions and understanding user behavior.

id.description: Unique identifier for this specific UserRecipeInteraction log entry.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who interacted with the recipe. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserRecipeInteraction)

recipeId.description: Reference to the Recipe that was suggested or interacted with. (Relationship: Recipe 1:N UserRecipeInteraction)

suggestedAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe was suggested to the user.

interactionType.description: The type of interaction the user had with the recipe (e.g., 'suggested', 'viewed', 'accepted', 'rejected', 'cooked').

feedbackRating.description: An optional numerical rating (e.g., 1-5 stars) provided by the user for the recipe.

feedbackComments.description: Optional textual feedback or comments from the user about the recipe.

pantryItemsConsumedIds.description: References to the specific PantryItem IDs that were marked as consumed or used as a result of accepting/cooking this recipe. (Relationship: UserRecipeInteraction N:N PantryItem)

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID `{userId}` matches Firebase Authentication UID. This ensures path-based ownership for secure, direct access. Authorization relies on `request.auth.uid` matching the document ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A global, canonical list of all possible food ingredients. Read-only for all authenticated users. Managed by the application/admin.

params.name: ingredientId
params.description: The unique ID of the ingredient.

definition.description: Stores specific instances of ingredients a user has in their pantry. This is a subcollection of a user's profile, making ownership explicit in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this pantry item.

params.name: pantryItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific pantry item instance.

definition.description: A global collection of AI-generated or curated recipes. Read-only for all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to the AI system or administrators.

params.name: recipeId
params.description: The unique ID of the recipe.

definition.description: A global, canonical list of kitchen appliances. Read-only for all authenticated users.

params.name: applianceId
params.description: The unique ID of the appliance.

definition.description: A global, canonical list of dietary restrictions. Read-only for all authenticated users.

params.name: restrictionId
params.description: The unique ID of the dietary restriction.

definition.description: A global, canonical list of food allergies. Read-only for all authenticated users.

params.name: allergyId
params.description: The unique ID of the allergy.

definition.description: A global, canonical list of taste preferences. Read-only for all authenticated users.

params.name: preferenceId
params.description: The unique ID of the taste preference.

definition.description: Logs individual user interactions with recipes, such as viewing, accepting, or cooking. Stored as a subcollection under a specific user to ensure path-based ownership. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who performed the interaction.

params.name: interactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the recipe interaction log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence & Denormalization:**
User-specific data such as `UserProfile`, `PantryItem`, and `UserRecipeInteraction` are stored in subcollections under `/users/{userId}/...`. This design inherently denormalizes the `userId` (owner's ID) directly into the document path. For instance, a `PantryItem` for a user will reside at `/users/{userId}/pantryItems/{pantryItemId}`. This placement makes the owner's identity (`userId`) directly accessible in Firestore Security Rules via the path wildcard (`userId`), eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to a parent document or to check a field within the document itself to determine ownership. This allows for atomic write operations for child documents and greatly simplifies security rules.

Global, canonical data like `Ingredient`, `Recipe`, `Appliance`, `DietaryRestriction`, `Allergy`, and `TastePreference` are placed in top-level collections (e.g., `/ingredients/{ingredientId}`). These collections represent shared, public datasets that all authenticated users can read, making them inherently 'authorization independent' as their access rules do not depend on user-specific attributes or complex lookups.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **For User-Owned Data:** The hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/pantryItems/{pantryItemId}` directly supports QAPs. A security rule like `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;` ensures that any `list` query from a client for `/users/{request.auth.uid}/pantryItems` will only ever retrieve documents owned by the authenticated user, without requiring additional client-side filtering or inefficient server-side filtering by the rules engine. The rule explicitly matches the requested path's `userId` with the authenticated user's ID, thereby implicitly filtering the data at the access layer.
2.  **For Global Data:** Collections like `/recipes` or `/ingredients` are designed for read-only access for all authenticated users. A simple rule `allow read: if request.auth != null;` allows all authenticated users to read and list all documents. This approach aligns with QAPs because the rule itself does not filter content based on user-specific data; rather, it grants or denies access to the entire collection based on authentication status, providing a consistent security posture for all documents within that collection.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
Collections are segregated based on their security posture. All documents within `/users/{userId}/pantryItems` have the same security requirement (owned by `userId`). Similarly, all documents in `/recipes` have the same security requirement (read-only for all authenticated users). This clear segregation prevents mixing data with different access needs, significantly simplifying the security rules. Path-based ownership is consistently used for user-specific data, while top-level collections are used for global, shared data, adhering to standard, predictable access patterns.
